name: PNW League Regulations
description: USTA Pacific Northwest Section league-specific regulations. Overrides national rules when applicable.
content:
1
USTA PNW
LEAGUE REGULATIONS
Updated 8/18/2025

2
1
1.02A WAIVER OF REGULATIONS AND PROCEDURES PROHIBITED.
1.02A(1)(PNWREG) – A waiver may be submitted for any USTA PNW Regulation. Waivers will be considered
based on the hardship of the specific situation, alternative options which have been tried or failed, and/or
how the waiver will benefit the Local League or PNW Section.
1.02B AMENDMENTS.
1.02B(1)(PNWREG) – PNW Regulations may be amended at any time by the USTA PNW Adult Competition
Committee with approval from the USTA/PNW Board of Directors.
1.02C WRITTEN INTERPRETATION.
1.02C(1) (PNW REG) – The final interpretation of any USTA/PNW Regulation will be determined by the USTA/
PNW Regulation Subcommittee and the USTA/PNW Section League Coordinator.
1.04A USTA LEAGUE PROGRAM.
1.04A(1) (PNW REG) – The USTA PNW has the option to provide the following additional USTA Leagues
(*denotes leagues without a Section Championship): Regional League Coordinators are also authorized to
introduce supplementary non-advancing Local Leagues, which are not listed in the chart below.
USTA LEAGUE PROGRAM ORGANIZATION AT THE PNW LEVEL
DIVISION 	AGE GROUP 	LEAGUE FORMAT 	NTRP LEVELS
Adult 	18 & Over 	1-Singles/2 Doubles 	2.5 Men, Open
Adult 	40 & Over 1-Singles/2 Doubles
or 3 Doubles 2.5, 5.0, Open
Adult 	65 & Over 	3-Doubles 	6.0, 7.0, 8.0, 9.0
Adult 	70 & Over* 	3-Doubles 	Open
Adult 	Tri-Level * 	3-Doubles 	4.5/4.0/3.5, 4.0/3.5/3.0
Adult 	Combo* 	3-Doubles 	5.5, 6.5, 7.5, 8.5, 9.5
Adult 	One Doubles 	1-Doubles 	2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0
Adult 	Flex Singles* 	1-Singles 	2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0
Mixed 	55 & Over 	3-Doubles 	6.0, 7.0, 8.0, 9.0
Mixed 	Coed* 	WTT 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0/6.0,
7.0, 8.0, 9.0
Mixed 	18 & Over 	3 Doubles 	5.0
Mixed 	40 & Over 	3 Doubles 	5.0
1.04B ADMINISTRATION.
1.04B(1)a (PNW REG) – The USTA PNW Adult Competition Committee shall assist to determine procedural
and certain operational matters with USTA League in the PNW. The committee members will be appointed
by the President of the USTA PNW Board of Directors.
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

3
2
1.04C OFFICIAL INFORMATION SYSTEM.
1.04C(1)a (PNW REG) - Players may be added to the roster at any time before the last two scheduled
matches of the season providing the team remains in compliance with all other regulations (Exception –
One Doubles, Mixed 55, Adult 65 – players may be added to the roster with one match remaining on the
local league schedule).
1.04C(1)b (PNW REG) - Single Team Leagues must comply with the local league registration deadline for
registering their team. Single Team Leagues may add players until the posted deadline. This only applies to
Adult 18 & Over (2.5 men, Open), Adult 40 & Over (2.5, 5.0, Open), Adult 65 & Over, Mixed 55 & Over, Coed
18-39, Coed and One Doubles Local Leagues.
1.04D LOCAL LEAGUE.
1.04D(4)a (PNW REG) - A team shall consist of players eligible to complete at a specific NTRP level of
competition in accordance with the following table.

USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

4
1.04E PLAYER ELIGIBILITY.
1.04E(1)a (PNW REG) – Pacific Northwest Local Area Boundaries
• 	Alaska Area
• 	Eastern Washington Area Includes: All points East of the Cascades, Northern Idaho north of the 45th
parallel, the Columbia River Gorge, and Baker, Gilliam, Grant, Hood River, Morrow, Sherman, Umatilla,
Wasco, Wallowa, Union, and Wheeler counties.
• 	Northwest Washington Area Includes: All points between Kent and Bellingham, including the greater
Seattle/Bellevue/Everett areas and Bainbridge Island (Members from Boeing Employees Tennis Club
that are in good standing are exempt from being considered out-of-area and may play as residents of
Northwest Washington.)
• 	Southwest Washington Area Includes: Pierce, Thurston, Lewis, Mason, Grays Harbor counties, Olympic
Peninsula & Kitsap County except Bainbridge plus So King County towns: Vashon, Federal Way, Auburn,
Pacific, Algona, Enumclaw (Bainbridge Island residents who are members in good standing at Kitsap
Tennis & Athletic Club may play out of Kitsap as a Southwest Washington resident.)
• 	Northern Oregon Area Includes: Greater Portland Metro Area, Vancouver, Longview, Camas
• 	Southern Oregon Area Includes: Salem, Albany, Corvallis, Salishan/Oregon Coast, Eugene,
Central Oregon, Klamath Falls, Medford, Roseburg, Coos Bay, Grants Pass, Ashland, Clatsop,
Tillamook counties.
1.04E(1)b (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season for Adult 18 & Over and Adult 40 & Over, no
more than four rostered players may reside outside the Local Area boundaries (no more than three rostered
players for 2.5 and 5.0 divisions).
1.04E(1)c (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season for One Doubles, no more than one rostered
players may reside outside the Local Area boundaries.
1.04E(1)d (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season for Coed 18-39, no more than two rostered
players may reside outside the Local Area boundaries.
1.04E(1)e (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season for all other leagues, no more than three
rostered players may reside outside the Local Area boundaries.
1.04E(1)f (PNW REG) – If no matches have been played by the ineligible players, the captain may choose
which player is removed from the roster. If matches have been played by all ineligible players then they are
removed based their registration date with the last player registered the first one removed. Matches played
by an ineligible player will be scored as a default.
1.04E(1)g (PNW REG) - Players must be prepared to show Government Issue photo I.D. when asked by USTA/
Pacific Northwest staff or designated volunteer.
1.04F OFFICIAL LEAGUE RATING PROGRAM.
1.04F(1)a (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season, Adult 18 & Over and Adult 40 & Over teams
must have 50% of the rostered players rated “in-level.” Exception(s):
a) Teams that advance to a National Championship are exempt from this rule for one year. (See
National Reg 2.06A(3)) Flights with only two teams must have 40% of the rostered players rated
in-level (See National Reg 1.04D(5)). This does not apply to individual line-ups in team matches.
b) Flights with only two teams must have 40% of the rostered players rated in-level (See National Reg
1.04D(5)). This does not apply to individual line-ups in team matches.
3
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

5
1.04F(1)b (PNW REG) - Throughout the entire league season, One Doubles teams must have 50% of the
rostered players rated “in-level.” In any One Doubles individual match, at least one doubles player must be
at level.
1.04F(1)c (PNW REG) - If no matches have been played by the ineligible players, the captain may choose
which players are removed from the roster. If matches have been played by all ineligible players the
player(s) are removed based on their registration date with the last player registered the first one removed.
1.04F(1)d (PNW REG) - For the Adult 18 & Over Open division, players must be rated at least 5.0
to participate.
1.04G PLAYER PARTICIPATION ELIGIBILITY.
1.04G(4)a (PNW REG) - Players may compete at more than one NTRP level in the same league within the
same local area.
2.01C COMPETITION FORMAT.
2.01C(3)a (PNW REG) - Captains must alert the area league coordinator immediately when defaulting an
entire team match. Total Team Defaults will be recorded in favor of the opponent and the standings will
reflect 6-0, 6-0 for all individual matches within a team default. All prior matches of a team that commits
a total team default will stand as played. Teams that default an entire team match will not advance to local
League Playoffs or a Sectional Championship.
2.01C(3)a1 (PNW REG) – For One Doubles, teams that default two entire team matches may
not advance to Local League Playoffs or a Sectional Championship. (Teams that default an
entire team match during Championship play will not be eligible to advance).
2.01C(3)b (PNW REG) - Individual matches which are not completed by the end of the season and prior to
playoffs, if any, will be recorded as a double default.
2.01C(3)c (PNW REG) – The following will be used for matches affected by inclement weather.
2.01C(3)c1 (PNW-REG) - Day(s) before or day of: When bad weather is known ahead of time, captains
are encouraged to work together and reschedule the match.
2.01C(3)c2 (PNW REG) - Day of and before the line-up exchange: If bad weather occurs before teams
have exchanged line-ups, captains may agree to “wait-out” the conditions or reschedule.
2.01C(3)c3 (PNW REG) - After the match(s) have begun: When bad weather forces a match(s) to be
postponed after they have begun, record scores on the scorecard as they stand. Both captains initial
both scorecards. Captain’s work together to reschedule the match. Once the scorecard is exchanged
it cannot be changed if all individual matches have already begun. If individual matches have not
started and are played on a different day then those individual matches may be changed on the
line-up card.
4
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

6
5
2.01C(3)e (PNW REG) - USTA PNW League Coordinators may use the Point Penalty System at their discretion
during any USTA League match in accordance with the Point Penalty System as described in Friend at
Court.
2.01C(4)a (PNW REG) - Best of three sets. In lieu of a 3rd set, a 10-point match tiebreak shall be played.
Regular scoring in all areas except Northwest Washington and Southwest Washington. No-ad will be used
in Northwest Washington and Southwest Washington for all local league matches. No-ad will be used for all
Northwest Washington playoff matches. Regular scoring will be used for all Southwest Washington playoff
matches.
2.01C(5)a (PNW REG) - Official Match Clock is indicated by a cell phone.
2.01C(5)b (PNW REG) - When a player is late in arriving for the player’s scheduled match time, and their
court is available, the captain shall start the lateness penalty clock. A player is not deemed to have arrived
until the player is properly clothed, equipped, and ready to play (If there is a circumstance/situation outside
of human control that is unpredictable and unpreventable, e.g., natural disasters, sudden infrastructure
failures, or sudden transportation interruption due to emergency, that makes it impossible for the majority
of a team to arrive on time the Regional League Coordinator may extend leniency). The lateness penalty is
as follows:
• 	5 minutes or less late: Loss of service toss plus one game
• 	5:01 – 10 minutes late: Loss of service toss plus 2 games
• 	10:01 or more minutes late: Default
* Late players receive the benefit of at least a 7-minute warm-up. Single Weekend Leagues and Local
League Playoff matches will be as follows, per Friend at Court, unless the Tournament Director or Referee
deems an exception for extenuating circumstances. (At the Tournament Director’s discretion: any individual
playing in two divisions regardless of whether they are currently playing in a match, and not available for a
called match may be assessed late penalties based on the Point Penalty System.)
• 	5 minutes or less late: Loss of service toss plus one game
• 	5:01 – 10 minutes late: Loss of service toss plus 2 games
• 	10:01 – 15 minutes late: Loss of service toss plus 3 games
• 	15 or more minutes late: Default
2.01C(6)a (PNW REG) - All players participating in the match must be listed online with TennisLink prior to
the initial exchange with the opposing captain. In the event a player is not listed, the point penalty and
individual match default procedures are to be used if play is delayed while waiting for an unregistered
player to register. All players must be registered before taking a court for warm-up.
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25
2.01C(3)d (PNW REG) - Line-Call Help. One Line Assistant to represent each team may be called to the court
at any time to assist players making line calls. If only one person is available, then one line assistant may be
used. Line Assistants cannot make a line call or foot fault call. They can confirm or over-rule a line call when
asked by a player. Line Assistants can confirm an egregious foot fault. Line Assistants may not interpret any
other rule of tennis and should stand quietly at both ends of the net. If two Line Assistants disagree on a
call a let is played.

7
2.02A LOCAL LEAGUE.
2.02A(1)a (PNW REG) - Team Match wins will be used to determine standings. If there is a tie in team match
wins, the following shall be used to break the tie in the order listed until the tie is broken: Individual Wins,
Head-to-Head, Least Sets Lost, Least Games Lost, Head-to-Head Individual Wins, Head-to-Head Least Sets
Lost, Head-to-Head Least Games Lost, Coin Flip.
2.02A(1)b (PNW REG) - For Adult 40 & Over league with 4 lines for an individual team match. If a team match
ends in an exact 2-2 tie, the following shall be used to break the tie in the order listed until the tie is broken:
Least Sets Lost, Least Games Lost. If there is still a tie, Tennislink is programmed to determine the winner as
the team which won the #1 doubles position.
2.02B CHAMPIONSHIPS.
2.02B(1)a (PNW REG) - All players must play two (2) matches during a multi-week regular season to be
eligible to play in Local League Playoffs (exception Adult 65 & Over, Mixed 55 & Over, and One Doubles one
(1) match required). One (1) default received may count as a match played. One (1) match is required for
Eastern Washington Area Championships and One Weekend Leagues.
(Number in parenthesis is the number of allowed defaulted matches that count toward eligibility.)
2.02B(1)b (PNW REG) - Single team leagues as designated in 1.04C(1)b may advance directly to Sectionals
6
PNW CHAMPIONSHIP ADVANCEMENT
To Advance To: 	Multi-Week Local League Playoff 	Sectional Championship
Adult 18 & Over 	2(1) 	2(1)
Adult 40 & Over 	2(1) 	2(1)
Adult 55 & Over 	2(1) 	2(1)
Mixed 18 & Over 	2(1) 	2(1)
Mixed 40 & Over 	2(1) 	2(1)
One Doubles 	1 (1) 	1 (1)
Adult 65 & Over 	1 (1) 	1 (1)
Mixed 55 & Over 	1 (1) 	1 (1)
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25
2.01C(6)b (PNW REG) - When match start times are split into more than one match start time on the same
day, the complete line-up (all 3, 4 or 5 lines) must be exchanged at the start time of the first match. Any
individual matches not exchanged at the first match start time will be defaults and recorded 6-0, 6-0
in favor of the opponent. If a team match is played on multiple days, then the remaining matches are
exchanged on the new date played.
and are exempt from rule 2.02B(1)a.

8
2.03A ELIGIBILITY.
2.03A(2)a(1) (PNW REG) - Players who qualify to play in a Sectional Championship in two or more Local
Areas at the same NTRP level must declare which team they will represent prior to the first match played
by any such team, regardless of whether or not the player is in the line-up. If a player does not declare
ahead of time, the first match played by that player in the Sectional Championship will be the team they
will represent. Players who qualify to play on two or more teams at different NTRP levels in a Sectional
Championship may compete on all teams.
2.03A(5)a (PNW REG) - At all Section Championships, a minimum number of team members as shown
in the following table, who were on the final roster of their local league team at the conclusion of local
league play, must individually register, be available, and eligible to compete or to combine and compete,
as appropriate. The required minimum number of team members available and eligible may be reduced
with an approved waiver, but to not less than the minimum as noted in the following table. Registration
minimums will not be waived.
7

USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

9
2.03E TEAM MATCH AND SCORING.
2.03E(a) (PNW REG) - A team match will consist of the matches as shown in the following table. The team
winning the majority of individual matches will be awarded one team point at PNW Section Championships.
For each team match, a majority of the individual matches must actually be played by the two teams to
constitute a valid team match. The Championship Committee has the authority to determine the actions to
take when the combination of individual match defaults given by the two teams in the team match would
result in a situation where the majority of the individual matches would not be played.
8
TEAM MATCH AND SCORING AT PNW SECTION CHAMPIONSHIPS

Adult 40 & Over 3.0,

a) Sets: Loser of the fewest number of sets.
b) Games: Loser of the fewest number of games.
c) Game Winning Percentage: Total games won divided
by total games played
d) Winner of No. 1 Doubles

Mixed 40 & Over 	3 Doubles 	2 Individual Matches Won
One Doubles 	One Doubles 	1 Individual Match Won

Mixed 55 & Over 	3 Doubles 	2 Individual Matches Won
2.03E(a) (PNW REG) – Line Assistants in Championships – Use Regulation 2.01C(3)d (PNW REG).
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

10

2.03I INDIVIDUAL DEFAULTS.
2.03I1 (PNW REG) - When a player is late in arriving for the player’s scheduled match time at a Sectional
Championship and his/her court is available, the Tournament Desk shall start the lateness penalty clock
unless the Tournament Director or Referee deems an exception for extenuating circumstances. (At the
Tournament Director’s discretion: any individual playing in two divisions regardless of whether they are
currently playing in a match, and not available for a called match may be assessed late penalties based
on the Point Penalty System.) A player is not deemed to have arrived until the player is properly clothed,
equipped, and ready to play. The lateness penalty is as follows per Friend at Court:
• 	5 minutes or less late: Loss of service toss plus one game
• 	5:01 – 10 minutes late: Loss of service toss plus 2 games
• 	10:01 – 15 minutes late: Loss of service toss plus 3 games
• 	15 or more minutes late: Default
• 	Late players receive the benefit of at least a 7-minute warm-up.
2.03K TEAM DEFAULTS.
2.03K(1)a (PNW REG) -TOTAL TEAM DEFAULT AT SECTION CHAMPIONSHIPS – A Grievance will be filed by
the SLC against Captain(s) and all rostered team members of the defaulting team. Team penalties may
include but are not limited to non-advancement, disqualification, or suspension.
9
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25

11
2.04B NTRP DYNAMIC DISQUALIFICATION PROCEDURES.
2.04B(2)a1 (PNW REG) - Dynamic rating calculations will be run throughout play at all Local League Playoffs.
2.04B(2)b1 (PNW REG) - Dynamic rating calculations will be run after the conclusion of all Sectional
Championships.
2.04E SCORING PROCEDURES.
2.04E(1)a (PNW REG) - During local league play, all matches played at the disqualified level will be reversed
in favor of the opponent, 6-0, 6-0. Players who are DNTRP disqualified may join a team at a higher level at
any time if otherwise eligible and the team remains in compliance with all other regulations.
2.05B YEAR-END COMPUTER RATINGS.
2.05B3(b)1 (PNW REG) – Players who are found to have valid computer ratings, after the appeal process,
that place them above the NTRP level at which they are competing may continue their participation at
the lower NTRP level through the conclusion of the Local League Playoffs). Players who are found to have
valid computer ratings, after the appeal process, that place them above the NTRP level at which they are
competing will not be allowed to participate at the lower NTRP level for the Sectional Championships.
2.05B3(c)1 (PNW REG) - Exception for 5.0 Mixed 18 & Over and Mixed 40 & Over Rosters Only
(Combined Ratings of 5.0): Players on 5.0 Mixed 18 & Over or Mixed 40 & Over Early Start teams
(combined ratings of 5.0) may continue participating through all championship levels unless their year-
end rating reaches the "clearly above level" threshold or they receive an automatic appeal up. If either
occurs, they must immediately adjust to their new NTRP level. Matches played prior to this adjustment
will remain valid.
2.06A MOVE UP/SPLIT UP
2.06A(2)a (PNW REG) – One Doubles Tri-Level Split-Up Requirement
Any One Doubles team that qualifies for or advances to the USTA Tri-Level National Invitational must
adhere to the following eligibility requirement for the subsequent championship year:
No player may be rostered on a One Doubles team with any teammate from the previous year’s advancing
team at the same NTRP level.
3.03A GENERAL PROCEDURES.
3.03A(1)a (PNW REG) – All grievances, except NTRP grievances, require a $50 fee payable to the USTA/
PNW. If a grievance is upheld, the $50 fee is returned. If the grievance is denied, the $50 fee will not be
returned.
3.03A(1)b (PNW REG) All league programs organized at the PNW Level will follow the USTA League
Suspension Point System and Grievance Process.
10
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25
3.03A(1)c (PNW REG) – Personal or third-party audio and video recordings are not accepted as evidence in the
USTA PNW grievance or grievance appeal process. USTA PNW may, at its discretion, review and consider its own
official security camera recordings. Only written firsthand accounts will be accepted for consideration.

11
3.03B LOCAL LEAGUE PROCEDURES.
3.03E NTRP GRIEVANCES.
3.03E(5)a (PNW REG) NTRP Grievances will be adjudicated after a championship event.
11
USTA PNW LEAGUE REGULATIONS – Update 6/10/25
3.03B(4)a (PNW REG) Scoring of Eligibility Disqualification in Local League. In the event of an eligibility
disqualification during the local league season and up to 14 days after the end of the local league
season, but prior to any playoff, all matches played by the disqualified player shall be considered
defaults and those matches shall be considered wins for the opposing players or doubles teams and
scored 6-0, 6-0 for determining standings.

12

TIMED MATCH PROCEDURES

Unfinished Matches (matches not completed at the end of court time, i.e., 75 minutes, 90 minutes)

A point in play as the timer goes off should be finished and counted as long as it results in a completed game.

Any unfinished game is tossed out (this includes a 2nd set tiebreak at 6-6 games all), and the resulting score either wins the match or, if tied, results in a final sudden-death point.

For Unfinished Matches, if the match ends under the following circumstances, the scoring is as follows. When entering scores on TennisLink, use the drop-down menu on the right to change from "Completed" to "Timed Match".

In the 3rd set 10-point tiebreak (also counts as a game and a set): The team ahead in the tiebreak wins (doesn't need to win by 2 points). If tied, play a sudden death point.

Split sets, with no time left to start the 3rd set tiebreak: Enter the scores for the first 2 sets. Whichever team has the most games won is declared the winner. If tied, play a sudden-death point.

Score at time limit examples:
5-4: "A" wins the match. They are ahead.
6-4, 3-0: "A" wins the match.
6-4, 0-2 or 7-6, 0-1: A sudden death point is played since each team has the same number of games won.
6-1, 4-3 or 7-6, 1-6: These are entered as timed matches. The teams with the most games won the match.
6-1, 4-4: "A" wins the match because they won more games. They have won 10 games vs. "B" with 5.
6-3, 6-7, (5-3): If teams have split sets and begun the 10-point match tiebreak, when time expires, the team ahead wins. If tied in points, a single point (deciding point) is played to decide the match in the same serving rotation.

For a single point (deciding point/sudden death point to determine a winner), serving rotation continues. The receiving team chooses which player will receive the deciding point for adult leagues. For mixed, it will still remain gender to gender.

If a sudden death point is to be used if the total number of games is tied - then the winner of that point gets the win on that court. Enter the number of games that were played, and select "timed match" from the drop-down menu.

Within the last 10 MINUTES of the match in the event a bathroom or Medical Time Out is needed - that court needs to STOP THE CLOCK. When the player returns, play resumes at the time they left the court.
